claims.evidence_text: In wheat crops, RUE is typically in the region of 2.8 g MJ −1 of photosynthetically active radiation (PAR, 400–700 nm)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat radiation-use efficiency is typically about 2.8 g/MJ of photosynthetically active radiation (PAR).

claims.evidence_text: In wheat crops, RUE is typically in the region of ... 1.4 g MJ −1 of solar radiation (Sinclair & Muchow, 1999 ).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat radiation-use efficiency is typically about 1.4 g/MJ of total solar radiation.
Thousand-grain weight of 12 CIMMYT spring wheat genotypes ranged from 35.2 to 51.6 g (means ~45 g) across raised-bed and flat-basin systems.

claims.evidence_text: HeightPM (cm) ... min B 90.1 F 88.3 ... max B 121.3 F 119.4 ; mean 105.7
claims.claim_summary: Plant height at physiological maturity of spring wheat genotypes ranged from 88.3 to 121.3 cm (mean ~105.7 cm).
Aboveground biomass at physiological maturity of spring wheat ranged from ~1192 to 1512 g/m2; ~7.6% higher in raised beds.

claims.evidence_text: The seed rate was 102 kg ha −1 for the 3 years in beds and 107 kg ha −1 in 2017–2018 and 2018–2019 and 106 kg ha −1 in 2019–2020 in flats.
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat seed rate was 102 kg/ha in raised beds and 106-107 kg/ha in flat basins.

claims.evidence_text: the wider row spacing in raised beds compared to flat basins (24 vs. 20 cm)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat row spacing was 24 cm in raised beds versus 20 cm in flat basins.

claims.evidence_text: Plants (m −2 ) ... min B 139 F 152 ... max B 180 F 201 ; mean B 156 F 173 ... nonsignificant difference (153–176 plants m −2 )
claims.claim_summary: Established plant density of spring wheat ranged from 139 to 201 plants/m2 (means 156-173).

claims.evidence_text: DTPM (DAE) mean B 117 F 114 ... min B 113 F 110 ... max B 121 F 118
claims.claim_summary: Days from emergence to physiological maturity of spring wheat ranged from 110 to 121 days (means 114-117 DAE).

claims.evidence_text: DTA (DAE) mean B 77 F 76 ... min B 72 F 72 ... max B 81 F 80
claims.claim_summary: Days from emergence to anthesis of spring wheat ranged from 72 to 81 days (means 76-77 DAE); grain-filling extended ~2 days in beds.

claims.evidence_text: fertilized with 50 kg N ha −1 (urea) during land preparation ... A second and third N application (200 and 50 kg N ha −1 , respectively, as urea) was applied at the first and second irrigation
claims.claim_summary: Total nitrogen applied to irrigated spring wheat was about 300 kg N/ha (50 at land prep, 200 and 50 at first/second irrigation) in 2017-2018.

claims.evidence_text: 50 kg N ha −1 (urea) during land preparation followed by 50 kg P ha −1 at sowing
claims.claim_summary: Phosphorus applied to irrigated spring wheat was 50 kg P/ha at sowing.

claims.evidence_text: fertilized with 50 kg N ha −1 (urea) during land preparation ... A second and third N application (200 and 50 kg N ha −1) was applied at the first and second irrigation, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Nitrogen for irrigated wheat was split: at land preparation, at first irrigation (largest dose), and at second irrigation.

claims.evidence_text: management advantages of raised beds compared with flat basins are as follows: reduced irrigation water requirements by 20%–40% ... reduced lodging
claims.claim_summary: Raised-bed planting of wheat is associated with reduced lodging compared with flat-basin planting.
